goal: The daily board's top-right chip showed a hardcoded 'Day 14' instead of the real streak
root_cause: The .daily-topbar__streak-chip markup in daily.html / th/daily.html shipped a static placeholder (num '14', label 'Day 14') and nothing in JS ever updated it — so every user saw 'Day 14'. (Streak is tracked LOCALLY in localStorage via progress.js getUserProgress().streak_current, so it's available signed-in or not.)
fix: Added renderStreakChip() in js/main.js (called from renderDaily after the #daily-board guard, no-op on /today/): reads getUserProgress().streak_current and writes the number + a localized 'day streak' label (dailyStreakLabel). streak===0 shows a gentle 'Start your streak' prompt (dailyStreakStart) with a ✦ instead of a sad 0. New i18n keys in js/common.js (EN/TH). Static HTML placeholder changed from the misleading '14'/'Day 14' to a neutral ✦ + 'day streak'/'วันต่อเนื่อง' so there's no flash of fake data. Note: the deck-NAME in the same topbar is ALSO hardcoded ('Velvet Familiar') and not wired to the active deck — separate latent bug, not fixed here.
verified: Local (chrome-devtools, cache-busted) EN+TH: streak=7 -> '7 day streak'; streak=0 -> '✦ Start your streak'; TH streak=12 -> '12 วันต่อเนื่อง'.
